product_updates.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
product_updates.content: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.

product_updates.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
product_updates.content: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

product_updates.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
product_updates.content: The episode has also intensified a broader debate in Washington over the influx of Chinese open models.

product_updates.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
product_updates.content: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

product_updates.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
product_updates.content: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

product_updates.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
product_updates.content: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting that the future of social networking lies in small, private communities powered by messaging, photo …

product_updates.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
product_updates.content: The company said it is reducing its headcount by 20%, or about 630 staff, to "support a leaner, more focused operating model" as it focuses on its AI Work Platform.

product_updates.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
product_updates.content: As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among U.S. companies, the arguing over what should be done about them has reached a fever pitch.

product_updates.title: Substack’s new tool tells you who’s been writing their newsletters with AI
product_updates.content: Substack is giving readers a way to estimate how much of a newsletter was written by AI, signaling a broader shift toward transparency around AI-assisted content.

product_updates.title: OpenAI’s AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
product_updates.content: OpenAI will spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Accelerating the frontiers of scientific discovery: Google’s $40M commitment to the Genesis Mission
technical_breakthroughs.content: Google commits $40M in AI tokens and credits for the Genesis Mission

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: NASA’s new space telescope and OpenAI’s autonomous hacker
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Shape-shifting mirrors on NASA’s new space telescope could unveil Jupiters like our own When NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope launches, as early as the…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Shape-shifting mirrors on NASA’s new space telescope could unveil Jupiters like our own
technical_breakthroughs.content: When NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope launches, as early as the end of next month, it will attempt one of astronomy’s most precise disappearing acts to date. The telescope will carry the first space-bound “active” coronagraph, an instrument that effectively erases most of the light from a…
